title: Minimalist Modern Bed
summary: A low platform bed with a clean plank headboard — built as four flat-packing sections (headboard, two rails, footboard) that screw together in the bedroom, in any mattress size.
description:
This bed is designed around a fact most bed plans ignore: a bed has to get INTO the bedroom. It builds as four separate flat sections — a pocket-hole-planked 1x8 headboard, two mirror-image side rails (2x6 with a 2x4 slat ledge and stub legs), and a low 2x6 footboard — that carry up the stairs one at a time and screw together in place. The same design disassembles for every future move, which is more than most store beds can say.

The cut list here is the QUEEN (60x80); the source publishes twin, full, king, and California king, and the parts scale simply — headboard planks at mattress width + 8", rails at mattress length, slats at mattress width. Sizes carried in the cut-list notes.

Two build details earn attention. First, the side rails' 2x4 ledge sits flush to the BOTTOM of the 2x6, creating the ledge the slats rest on — build the two rails in MIRROR, not as copies, or one ledge faces the wrong way. Second, the source's assembly trick: mark and PRE-DRILL the headboard's rail-connection holes at the workshop bench, so bedroom assembly is just driving screws into holes that already line up. Finish all four sections BEFORE assembly, flat, for the same reason.

A center 2x4 rail with its own leg carries the slat span — with it, 1x4 slats are plenty for a queen. Skip the center support and the bed will remind you every night why it was in the plan.

tools.note: 3/4" setting for the headboard planks, 1-1/2" for the footboard legs.
Pre-drill the headboard connection holes at the bench — future-you assembles in the bedroom.

materials.name: 1x8, 6 ft
materials.note: Headboard planks. Queen quantities throughout — see cut-list notes for other sizes.

materials.name: 1x4, 10 ft
materials.note: Bed slats — each 10-footer yields two queen slats.

materials.name: Stain or paint
materials.note: Applied to the four sections FLAT, before assembly.

cutList.note: Queen. Twin 46", full 62", king 84", cal king 80".
Cut to match your actual glued-up plank width.
About 50" — cut to fit the assembled panel.
Mattress length. 75" for twin/full, 84" cal king.
Flush to the BOTTOM of each rail — and the rails are MIRRORED.
Queen. 41" twin, 57" full, 79" king, 75" cal king.
Mattress width. Cal king wants 11 at 72".

steps.title: Panel up the headboard
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes along one edge of six of the seven 1x8s, about every 10", and join the planks face-flush with 1-1/4" pocket screws. Clamp each seam flat as you drive — the headboard is the face of this bed and a stepped seam will catch light forever.

steps.title: Frame the headboard back
steps.body:
Cut the 1x3 top rail to match the panel's real length and pocket-screw it across the top, holes inward. Cut the three verticals to fit and attach the same way. The back frame is what keeps seven planks acting as one panel.

Sand and finish the headboard NOW, flat on sawhorses.

steps.title: Build the side rails — in mirror
steps.body:
Screw a 2x4 ledge to each 2x6 rail, flush to the BOTTOM edge, with 2-1/2" screws — the step up from ledge to rail top is what hides the mattress edge. Add a 2x6 stub leg at the end of each ledge, square to the rail.

Build the second rail as a MIRROR of the first; two copies give you one rail with its ledge facing out of the bed. Then hold each rail against the headboard, trace the joint, and PRE-DRILL the connection holes — bedroom assembly becomes screwdriver work.

steps.title: Footboard and center rail
steps.body:
Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes in one end of each 6" footboard leg and attach them to the footboard, inset 1-1/2" from the ends, flush to the bottom — 2-1/2" pocket screws. Build the center rail: 2x4 with a 12" block at each end and the 2x6 center leg.

Finish these flat too. Every part of this bed gets its finish before the bedroom sees it.

steps.title: Assemble in the bedroom
steps.body:
Carry the four sections in. Screw the rails to the headboard through the pre-drilled holes, attach the footboard to the rail legs, and screw the center rail to headboard and footboard — all with the 2-1/2" star-drive screws.

Lay the ten slats across the ledges and center rail with even spacing and fasten each with the 1-5/8" screws. Mattress on. The frame comes apart with the same screwdriver the next time you move — which is the entire point.

images.alt: Low minimalist platform bed with plank headboard
